name: Chip
description: Chips are compact elements that represent an input, a filter, or a choice — a selected driver, an active filter on a vehicle list, a quick action. They pack a lot of interaction into a small footprint.
keyRule: Use chips for selections and filters the operator manipulates. For read-only status (Active/Idle/Offline) use a status chip with a consistent colour mapping.

variants.name: Input chip
variants.whenToUse: Represents a discrete entered value (selected drivers, tags) — usually removable.

variants.name: Filter chip
variants.whenToUse: Toggles a filter on a list; shows a selected state.

variants.name: Choice chip
variants.whenToUse: Single selection from a small inline set.

variants.name: Status chip (read-only)
variants.whenToUse: Conveys record status with the consistent colour mapping.

anatomy.name: Selected state
anatomy.description: check/tint indicating the chip is on.

behaviors.name: Selection & removal
behaviors.description: Filter/choice chips toggle on click; input chips can be removed via the trailing × or Backspace when focused.

behaviors.name: Overflow
behaviors.description: When many chips are present, wrap to multiple lines or provide a "+N more" affordance.

behaviors.name: Status mapping
behaviors.description: Status chips map state to colour consistently (Active=success, Idle=warning, Offline=error) and always keep the text label.

props.name: label
props.description: The value or option text. Keep labels short.

props.name: variant
props.description: Chip category per the spec's Variants table.

props.name: selected
props.description: Toggled/selected state for filter and choice chips.

props.name: status
props.description: Status colour mapping — only meaningful when variant="status" (Active=success, Idle=warning, Offline=error).

props.name: removable
props.description: Shows a trailing remove (×) control — typically used on input chips.

props.name: onRemove
props.description: Handler for the trailing remove control.

props.name: onClick
props.description: Click handler for interactive chip variants.

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the chip (renders at 38% opacity per spec).

doThis: Use chips for filters/selections the user controls.
Keep status chips read-only with consistent colours + labels.
dontDoThis: Don't use a chip as a primary button.
Don't encode status by colour alone.
Don't cram long text into a chip — keep labels short.
accessibility: Interactive chips are focusable with clear roles.
Selected state is exposed (e.g. aria-pressed) and removal is keyboard-operable.
Status chips include text, never colour alone.

related.name: Button
related.why: Primary commands are buttons, not chips
